title: lannea coromandelica
intro: Lannea coromandelica, also known as the Indian plum or desert date, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to the tropical regions of India and Sri Lanka. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Lannea coromandelica include its ability to treat various ailments such as diarrhea, fever, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
